title: Cyphostemma zechianum
taxonomy: Cyphostemma zechianum is a species of flowering plant in the grape family, Vitaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and is often found growing in rocky areas and woodland. This plant is part of the genus Cyphostemma, which is a group of plants closely related to the genus Vitis, commonly known as grapes. The classification of Cyphostemma zechianum places it within the family Vitaceae and its order, Rosales. Further classification includes the division Magnoliophyta and the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Cyphostemma zechianum has been traditionally used in medicine due to its reported antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin infections, and fever. In some regions, it is used to treat digestive issues and as a natural remedy for coughs. The plant's antiseptic properties make it useful for treating wounds and preventing infection. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Cyphostemma zechianum.

side_effects: Cyphostemma zechianum, also known as the "living stone," is a succulent that is native to South Africa. While it is a popular ornamental plant, there is limited information available on the potential side effects of the plant. However, it's essential to note that ingesting any plant parts can be toxic, and further research is needed to determine its safety for human consumption.
